title: wikstroemia pauciflora var. phymatoglossa
side_effects: Wikstroemia pauciflora var. phymatoglossa can have side effects if used improperly, such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and gastrointestinal problems. Ingesting large amounts of the plant can cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Prolonged exposure to the plant's essential oils may also lead to headaches, dizziness, and respiratory issues in some individuals. Additionally, improper use of the plant may interact with certain medications, exacerbating conditions such as hypertension and diabetes.
